parameters.text:
=You are an expert social media manager for Intuz's Twitter account. Generate engaging tweets for C-suite executives, CTOs, and engineering leaders.

🚨 ABSOLUTE CHARACTER LIMIT: 250 CHARACTERS MAXIMUM (including ALL text, spaces, hashtags, URLs)

MANDATORY PROCESS:
1. Draft tweet
2. Count EVERY character (letters, spaces, punctuation, hashtags, URLs)
3. If >250 characters: Shorten immediately
4. Recount until ≤250 characters
5. Never exceed 250 under any circumstances
6. Check for existing same tweets from the Google Sheet, so as to avoid duplicate tweets

OUTPUT FORMAT:
{
"tweet_content": "[Your tweet here - max 280 chars]",
"character_count": "[X/280]",
"status": "Published"
}

CONTENT FOCUS: AI trends, automation, cloud tech, RAG, ML innovations, MCP, n8n, Software Development

REQUIREMENTS:
- Always include #Intuz
- Add 4-7 relevant hashtags
- Include URL for https://www.mlopscrew.com/ if the tweet is cloud related otherwise add https://www.intuz.com/
- Ask questions or add CTAs
- Make each tweet unique
- Keep tone: innovative, authoritative, educational

CHARACTER BUDGET:
- Core message: ~150-180 chars
- Hashtags: ~40-60 chars
- URL: ~20-30 chars
- TOTAL: ≤250 chars

TARGET: Drive leads, engagement, "Contact Us" inquiries

FINAL CHECK: Confirm character count ≤280 before submitting!

nodes.name: Add new Tweet to Google sheet

parameters.content:
## Need Help? 

We develop tailored workflow to save youe valuable time. Should you have any questions or wish to explore more custom automation solutions, we would be happy to connect


### Email: getstarted@intuz.com
### Website: https://www.intuz.com/

### Step 1: Scheduler Trigger  
This node acts as the workflow’s timer ⏱️.  
It is configured to automatically trigger the workflow every **6 hours**, ensuring that new tweet content is generated and posted consistently without requiring manual input.

### Step 2: AI Agent Node  
This node acts as the **content brain 🧠** of the workflow.  
It uses a carefully designed prompt to control the tweet generation process, ensuring that each post is relevant, professional, and aligned with the intended tone. The AI agent dynamically adapts content based on the given context, making every tweet unique and impactful.

### Step 2.1: AI Agent Node  
This node acts as the **content brain 🧠** of the workflow.  
It uses a carefully designed prompt to control the tweet generation process, ensuring that each post is relevant, professional, and aligned with the intended tone. The AI agent dynamically adapts content based on the given context, making every tweet unique and impactful.

### Step 2.2: Simple Memory Node  
This node acts as a **context store 🧠**, holding the conversation history and relevant details passed from the AI Agent and Chat Model.  
It ensures continuity so that future tweet generations stay consistent in tone, style, and context.

### Step 2.3: Get Rows from Google Sheet Node  
This node retrieves the list of **already published tweets 📑** from a connected Google Sheet.  
By keeping track of past posts, it ensures the workflow avoids publishing duplicate tweets and maintains fresh, engaging content each time.

### Step 3: Code Node  
This node parses the **raw string response 📝** generated by the AI model into a clean, structured **JSON format**.  
By converting the response into executable JSON, it ensures that the tweet content is easy to process and ready for further automation steps.

### Step 4: X Create Tweet Node  
This node publishes the final tweet 🐦 generated by the AI directly to **X (Twitter)**.  
It ensures that the content parsed and validated in earlier steps is posted automatically on schedule, keeping your feed consistent and engaging.

### Step 5: Add New Tweet to Google Sheet Node  
This node logs each newly published tweet into the connected **Google Sheet**.  
It records both the **tweet content** and its **status** (marked as *Published*) to maintain a structured history of all posts.  

This log is later referenced in **Step 2.3** (Get Rows from Google Sheet) to check for duplicates, ensuring that the AI never regenerates or reposts the same tweet — completing the cycle of tweet creation, publication, and validation.

### Prerequisites  

#### Google Sheets  
- Connect your Google credentials with **Google Sheets** and **Google Drive** services enabled.  
- Create a Google Sheet with the following column headers:  
  - **Tweet Content**  
  - **Status**  
- Use this sheet in all Google Sheet nodes within the workflow.  

#### OpenAI  
- Add and connect your **OpenAI API Key** in the **OpenAI Chat Model node** to enable tweet generation.  

#### X (Twitter)  
- Connect your **Twitter (X) account credentials** in the **Create Tweet node** to allow automated posting.

parameters.text: ✅ Tweet posted successfully! Check your Twitter feed.
❌ Post cancelled. No tweet was published.

main.node: Add new Tweet to Google sheet
